div.p: Strategy: Every new chunk has the same number of blocks as the initial allocation. blocks_per_chunk = initial_blocks (constant)
Strategy: Each new chunk doubles in size. Like std::vector growth. blocks_per_chunk = previous_total_capacity (doubles each time)
Predictable failure: System runs until memory exhausted, then gracefully returns NULL. Easy to handle, easy to test.
Unpredictable failure: Small request triggers massive allocation attempt. May fail even when plenty of memory exists (fragmentation).

g.text: ⚠ **Accept OOM risk** — large allocations may fail
⚠ **Accept waste** — up to 50% of last chunk unused
